initial_program:
% ---------- Grid dimensions ----------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% ---------- Direct copy ----------
output(R, C, Color) :- input(R, C, Color).

% ---------- Rotated output (rotate 90 anticlockwise, then gravity)
output(Rout, Cout, Color) :-
    input(Rin, Cin, Color),
    RotR = M - 1 - Cin,          % new row before gravity
    RotC = Rin,                  % new column before gravity
    Rout = RotR + Rin,           % gravity drops the shape to the lowest free rows
    Cout = RotC,
    Rout >= 0, Rout < N,
    Cout >= 0, Cout < M,
    not input(Rout, Cout, _).   % place rotated cells only on originally empty cells

% ---------- Exactly one colour per output cell ----------
output_cell(R, C) :- row(R), col(C).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

#show output/3.

% ---------- Grid dimensions ----------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% ---------- Direct copy ----------
output(R, C, Color) :- input(R, C, Color).

% ---------- Rotated output (rotate 90 anticlockwise, then gravity)
rotR(Rin, Cin, RotR) :- input(Rin, Cin, Color), RotR = M - 1 - Cin.
rotC(Rin, Cin, RotC) :- input(Rin, Cin, Color), RotC = Rin.
output(Rout, Cout, Color) :- rotR(Rin, Cin, RotR), rotC(Rin, Cin, RotC), Rout = RotR + Rin, Cout = RotC, Rout >= 0, Rout < N, Cout >= 0, Cout < M, not input(Rout, Cout, _).

% ---------- Exactly one colour per output cell ----------
output_cell(R, C) :- row(R), col(C).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).
